changesProposed.name: OpenAI

current.name: OpenAI
current.founder: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever

verified.name: OpenAI
verified.description: An AI research and deployment company that developed the GPT family of large language models, DALL-E series of text-to-image models, and ChatGPT. OpenAI consists of a nonprofit foundation and a for-profit public benefit corporation.
verified.notes: Valuation updated to $500B based on October 2025 share sale ($6.6 billion) per Wikipedia source. Company transitioned from nonprofit to for-profit capped entity in 2019, restructured in 2025 to public benefit corporation with nonprofit foundation holding 26% ownership.

changes.current: Sam Altman, Greg Brockman, Ilya Sutskever
changes.verified: Elon Musk, Sam Altman, Ilya Sutskever, Greg Brockman, Trevor Blackwell, Vicki Cheung, Andrej Karpathy, Durk Kingma, John Schulman, Pamela Vagata, Wojciech Zaremba

changesProposed.name: SpaceX

verified.name: SpaceX
verified.description: American aerospace and artificial intelligence company that develops reusable launch vehicles, spacecraft, and satellite constellation technology. As of 2026, SpaceX conducts more orbital launches annually than any other launch provider.
verified.notes: Wikipedia states SpaceX is expected to have an IPO in 2026, but database entry claims it is preparing for IPO at $1.5T valuation—the $1.5T valuation figure could not be verified in sources. Company is described as private, not Series G. Founded in El Segundo, California in 2002 but now headquartered in Starbase, Texas.

changesProposed.name: Starcloud

current.name: Starcloud
current.founder: Philip Johnston, Ezra Feilden, Adi Oltean

verified.name: Starcloud
verified.description: A United States–based company that designs, builds, and deploys data centers in space using proprietary technology. The company launched its first test satellite, Starcloud-1, equipped with an Nvidia H100 GPU in November 2025.
verified.notes: Company was originally founded as 'Lumen Orbit' in El Segundo, California in January 2024, relocated to Redmond, Washington in February 2024, and rebranded to Starcloud in March 2025 following a legal challenge from Lumen Technologies. On March 30, 2026, announced Series A funding of $170M at $1.1B valuation, making it the fastest company to reach unicorn status in Y Combinator history at 17 months after completing the program.
